question: [File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.CONTRACTID] Can an unprivileged external user supply filterKeys.CONTRACTID with an EVM address during a partition to cause a contract lookup that hangs on a degraded DB node, holding a connection open and contributing to pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.CONTRACT_ID] Can an unprivileged external user supply filterKeys.CONTRACT_ID with queryParamOperators.gte set to 0 during a partition to trigger a full contract table scan on a degraded replica, causing the query to time out?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.TOPIC_ID] Can an unprivileged external user supply filterKeys.TOPIC_ID during a partition to cause a topic message lookup that hangs on a degraded DB node, holding a connection open and contributing to pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.ENTITY_PUBLICKEY] Can an unprivileged external user supply filterKeys.ENTITY_PUBLICKEY during a partition to trigger the zeroRandomPageCostQueryHint path, which opens a dedicated DB client connection that is never released if the partition causes the commit to fail, leaking the connection?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.ID_OR_ALIAS_OR_EVM_ADDRESS] Can an unprivileged external user supply filterKeys.ID_OR_ALIAS_OR_EVM_ADDRESS with a ZERO_EVM_ADDRESS value during a partition to cause an entity lookup that returns no results, which is cached as an authoritative 404, causing clients to incorrectly believe the account does not exist?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: EvmAddressType.ANY] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address that matches EvmAddressType.ANY during a partition to cause an ambiguous entity resolution that queries both shard/realm and no-shard/realm paths, doubling the DB load on a degraded replica?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: EvmAddressType.NUM_ALIAS] Can an unprivileged external user supply a long-zero EVM address (EvmAddressType.NUM_ALIAS) during a partition to cause a numeric alias lookup that hangs on a degraded DB node, holding a connection open and contributing to pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: EvmAddressType.NO_SHARD_REALM] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address without shard/realm (EvmAddressType.NO_SHARD_REALM) during a partition to cause a lookup that falls back to a secondary DB path, doubling the query load on a degraded replica?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: EvmAddressType.OPTIONAL_SHARD_REALM] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address with optional shard/realm (EvmAddressType.OPTIONAL_SHARD_REALM) during a partition to cause an ambiguous lookup that queries multiple DB paths, amplifying the load on a degraded replica?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: ZERO_EVM_ADDRESS] Can an unprivileged external user supply ZERO_EVM_ADDRESS as an account identifier during a partition to cause a lookup that returns no results, which is cached as an authoritative 404, causing clients to incorrectly believe the zero address account does not exist?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: ZERO_UINT256] Can an unprivileged external user supply ZERO_UINT256 as a contract slot value during a partition to cause a storage lookup that returns no results, which is cached as authoritative, misleading clients about contract state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: EVM_ADDRESS_LENGTH (20)] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address that is exactly EVM_ADDRESS_LENGTH (20) bytes but contains all zeros during a partition to cause a lookup that returns no results, which is cached as authoritative, misleading clients about account existence?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: ETH_HASH_LENGTH (32)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transaction hash that is exactly ETH_HASH_LENGTH (32) bytes but is all zeros during a partition to cause a lookup that returns no results, which is cached as authoritative, misleading clients about transaction existence?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: HEX_PREFIX ('0x')] Can an unprivileged external user supply a value with HEX_PREFIX ('0x') stripped during a partition to cause a hex parsing error that results in an unhandled exception and a 500 response that leaks internal path information?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: AUTO_RENEW_PERIOD_MULTIPLE] Can an unprivileged external user supply an auto-renew period value that, after multiplication by AUTO_RENEW_PERIOD_MULTIPLE (BigInt(1e9)), overflows the MAX_LONG boundary during a partition, causing an unhandled BigInt exception and a 500 response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: WEIBARS_TO_TINYBARS (10_000_000_000n)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a weibar value that, after division by WEIBARS_TO_TINYBARS, produces a fractional tinybar amount during a partition, causing incorrect fee calculations to be cached as authoritative responses?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: EMPTY_STRING] Can an unprivileged external user supply an empty string for filterKeys.ACCOUNT_ID during a partition to cause a query that bypasses entity ID validation and returns all accounts, which are then cached as an authoritative response for the empty-string key?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: apiPrefix ('/api/v1')] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to paths that differ from apiPrefix ('/api/v1') by case or trailing slash during a partition to bypass the response cache and force fresh DB queries on a degraded replica, amplifying the partition impact?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: contentTypeHeader] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted Content-Type header during a partition to cause the response cache to store a response with an incorrect content type, causing clients to misparse the cached response after the partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: requestIdLabel] Can an unprivileged external user observe the requestIdLabel in error responses during a partition to correlate multiple requests and infer the internal request processing order, enabling timing-based attacks on the partitioned system?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: requestPathLabel] Can an unprivileged external user observe the requestPathLabel in error responses during a partition to infer internal routing paths that are not publicly documented, enabling targeted attacks on specific endpoints?
